message.content: What's the cleanest way to rotate the Slack webhook URL stored in Vault — I don't want to bounce anything that's currently using it.

content.text: Use Vault's KV-v2 versioning: write the new URL as the next version of the same secret path, then update the consumers to read the latest version (they're already pinned to `latest` if you didn't override). For services that cache the secret in process memory (most of them), trigger a SIGHUP / restart on a rolling basis — `kubectl rollout restart` if you're on k8s, ECS service redeploy otherwise. Vault keeps the previous version available so anything still holding the old URL keeps working until the slow restart catches up. Audit log will show the version-change event.
